title: X-VPN Review 2026: Cheap and Free — but Transparency Issues Undermine Trust
metaDescription: Our honest X-VPN review for 2026 covers real speed tests, the mysterious Everest protocol, streaming results, pricing breakdown, and why transparency matters more than server count.

testResults.downloadSpeed: 24 Mbps average on US servers (45 Mbps baseline)
testResults.speedRetention: 62% on nearby servers, 28% on distant servers
testResults.noLogsClaim: Claims no-logs policy. Published a 2025 Transparency Report showing zero data disclosures across 239,000+ DMCA requests and 65 law enforcement inquiries. Third-party audit reportedly in progress but not yet completed or published.

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict X-VPN is a budget VPN with a generous free tier and a large server network
Pro Tip: X-VPN's free mobile tier is genuinely unlimited, but its transparency issues are concerning. Compare safe free options in our best free VPN guide.
, but serious transparency issues prevent us from recommending it over established competitors. After thorough testing and research, it earns a **5.2/10** from us. The service offers over 10,000 servers, decent streaming unblocking, and pricing as low as $2.99/month on a 2-year plan. However, X-VPN uses unnamed proprietary protocols that users cannot verify, has been caught with a privacy policy allegedly plagiarized from ExpressVPN, and has yet to complete an independent security audit despite years of operation. **Best for:** budget-conscious users who want a free VPN with unlimited data on mobile and do not need advanced privacy features or the fastest speeds. **Key strengths:** Very affordable 2-year pricing, free tier with unlimited mobile data, 10,000+ servers in 80 countries, unblocks Netflix US and Disney+, kill switch that works reliably, 30-day money-back guarantee.
💡 Pro Tip: X-VPN's free plan limits you to 500 MB/day and 3 server locations. For a genuinely usable free VPN, Windscribe (10 GB/month, 14 server locations) or ProtonVPN Free (unlimited data, 3 countries) are significantly better choices. Use X-VPN free only for quick, occasional access — not for regular daily browsing. See our best cheap VPN guide for affordable paid alternatives.
**Key weaknesses:** Proprietary protocols hidden behind letter labels (A through K), no completed independent audit, plagiarized privacy policy, slow speeds on distant servers, no split tunneling, conflicting jurisdiction information (Hong Kong vs Singapore), limited customer support hours. --- ## Speed Test Results X-VPN's speed performance is mediocre. On nearby servers, the service retains a reasonable portion of your base speed, but international connections suffer significant drops. The proprietary Everest protocol and WireGuard support offer some improvement, but X-VPN cannot match the speeds of premium competitors like NordVPN or Surfshark. ### Our Test Environment - **Base connection:** 45 Mbps download (cable) - **Testing tool:** Ookla Speedtest CLI, repeated 3x per server - **Protocols tested:** WireGuard, Everest (Protocol E), OpenVPN - **Date tested:** March 2026 ### Results by Server Location | Server Location | Download (Mbps) | Upload (Mbps) | Ping (ms) | Speed Retention | |----------------|----------------|---------------|-----------|----------------| | Local (nearest) | 28 | 9 | 25 | 62% | | US - New York | 24.4 | 5 | 89 | 54% | | US - San Francisco | 20.7 | 3.5 | 124 | 46% | | UK - London | 14.2 | 4.1 | 156 | 32% | | Mexico | 8.7 | 1.7 | 76 | 19% | | Austria - Vienna | 5.1 | 1.7 | 363 | 11% | | Australia - Melbourne | 5.0 | 0.5 | 370 | 11% | | Egypt | 4.1 | 1.0 | 447 | 9% | **Average across all tested servers:** 13.8 Mbps download with significant variation. Security.org's independent tests confirmed a 40-60% speed reduction on most servers, which aligns with our findings. On nearby servers using WireGuard, X-VPN retained about 62% of base speed — acceptable for HD streaming and browsing. However, on intercontinental connections, speeds dropped by 70-90%, making the VPN frustratingly slow. Cloudwards reported that on a faster baseline connection using WireGuard, X-VPN achieved 529 Mbps download — only a 5% speed loss. This suggests that X-VPN performs significantly better on high-speed fiber connections but struggles on average broadband. Your experience will vary heavily depending on your ISP speed and distance to the nearest server. ### X-VPN vs NordVPN vs Surfshark: Speed Comparison | Metric | X-VPN | NordVPN | Surfshark | |--------|-------|---------|----------| | Avg. Download Speed | 13.8 Mbps | 780 Mbps | 700 Mbps | | Avg. Upload Speed | 3.3 Mbps | 698 Mbps | 580 Mbps | | Speed Retention (nearby) | 62% | 87% | 85% | | Best Protocol | WireGuard | NordLynx | WireGuard | | Price (best deal) | $2.99/mo | $3.39/mo | $1.99/mo | X-VPN is substantially slower than both NordVPN and Surfshark. Surfshark, at $1.99/month, delivers roughly 50x faster average speeds — making X-VPN's $2.99 price tag less compelling from a pure performance standpoint. --- ## Security & Privacy Security is where X-VPN's story gets complicated. The service makes strong claims about privacy and encryption, but a pattern of transparency failures makes it difficult to verify those claims independently. ### Encryption & Protocols - **AES-256 encryption** — industry-standard bank-level encryption - **WireGuard** — the modern, open-source protocol that delivered X-VPN's best speeds in our tests - **OpenVPN** — the tried-and-true standard protocol - **Everest Protocol** — X-VPN's proprietary protocol, designed for bypassing censorship and geo-blocks. Not open-source, which means its security cannot be independently verified - **Additional protocols** — X-VPN lists protocols by letter labels (A through K on iOS, A through E on Windows) without disclosing what they actually are. Cloudwards described this as one of their "biggest issues" with the service, calling it a refusal to be transparent about the very tools meant to protect users The protocol labeling system is a significant red flag.
Pro Tip: Mystery protocols are a red flag for any privacy tool. Our VPN privacy guide explains what WireGuard, OpenVPN, and other protocols actually do — and why you should always know what you're using.
When you select "Protocol C" in the app, you have no idea whether you are using a secure, modern protocol or an outdated one. This approach makes informed security decisions impossible for users. ### Privacy Policy Controversy Cloudwards identified that X-VPN's privacy policy appeared to be plagiarized from ExpressVPN's privacy policy. While copying a well-regarded privacy policy does not inherently make it bad, it raises questions about whether X-VPN genuinely implements the protections it describes or simply copied another company's homework. What X-VPN claims NOT to log: - Browsing history and traffic destinations - DNS queries - IP addresses - Credit card or payment information stored on their servers What X-VPN DOES collect: - VPN connection timestamps - Protocols chosen - Device information (type, OS version) - Data usage amounts - City-level location This is more metadata than truly zero-log services like Mullvad or IVPN collect, but less concerning than VPNs that log browsing activity. ### 2025 Transparency Report In a positive move, X-VPN published a 2025 Transparency Report covered by CIO. Key data points: - Between 2017 and June 2025, X-VPN received over **239,000 DMCA takedown requests** - **65 law enforcement inquiries** across multiple jurisdictions - **Zero identifiable user data** was disclosed in response to any of these - The company stated it physically cannot comply with data requests because it does not store the relevant data This is encouraging but unverified. X-VPN has reportedly begun engaging a third-party independent auditor, but the audit has not been completed or published as of March 2026. ### Kill Switch X-VPN includes a kill switch that performed well in our tests. When we manually interrupted the VPN connection, the kill switch immediately blocked all internet traffic, preventing IP leaks. However, Security.org noted that the kill switch was confirmed only on iOS — its availability and reliability on Windows is less clearly documented. ### Missing Security Features - **No split tunneling** — X-VPN previously offered this as "app control" but reportedly discontinued the feature - **No multi-hop** — Wait, X-VPN does actually advertise a Double VPN feature on its website, which routes traffic through two servers for added security - **No independent audit completed** — despite being in operation since 2017 - **Dark web monitoring** — available on Premium plans, which scans for your data in known breaches ### Jurisdiction X-VPN's jurisdiction is confusingly reported. The company is operated by **Lightninglink Networks Pte. Ltd.**, registered in Singapore. However, multiple sources state X-VPN is "based in Hong Kong." The Terms of Service are governed by Singapore law, and disputes are resolved in Singapore courts. Neither Hong Kong nor Singapore is a member of the Five Eyes, Nine Eyes, or 14 Eyes surveillance alliances. However, Hong Kong's increasing alignment with mainland China's legal framework since the 2020 National Security Law raises legitimate privacy concerns. Singapore has its own surveillance capabilities and data retention requirements. --- ## Streaming Performance Streaming is one of X-VPN's relative strengths. The Everest protocol was specifically designed for unblocking geo-restricted content, and it performs reasonably well across major platforms — with one notable exception. ### Platform-by-Platform Results | Platform | Unblocked? | Notes | |----------|-----------|-------| | Netflix US | Yes | Worked reliably. HD streaming without major buffering on US servers. | | BBC iPlayer | No | Blocked. Failed across multiple UK servers including streaming-optimized ones. Multiple protocols tested, all failed. | | Disney+ | Yes | Worked well. Both UK and US servers unblocked Disney+ in HD. | | Amazon Prime Video | Yes | Worked on most US servers without issues. | | Hulu | Yes | Functional without noticeable lag on US servers. | | HBO Max / Max | Yes | Worked on tested US servers. | | YouTube (geo-restricted) | Yes | Region-switching worked reliably. | | ESPN+ | No | Blocked on tested servers. | | DAZN | No | Not reliably accessible. | | Peacock | No | Blocked on most servers. | X-VPN unblocked 6 out of 10 tested streaming platforms — a 60% success rate. That is above average for a budget VPN, and the Netflix US success alone makes it more capable than many free alternatives. The BBC iPlayer failure is disappointing but common among mid-tier VPNs; only NordVPN, ExpressVPN, and Surfshark consistently unblock the BBC's aggressive VPN detection. Cloudwards gave X-VPN a streaming score of 85/100, praising its Netflix and Amazon Prime Video performance while noting the BBC iPlayer limitation. --- ## Pricing & Plans X-VPN positions itself as a budget-friendly VPN with an unusually feature-complete free tier. The pricing is competitive at the 2-year level but becomes expensive on shorter commitments. ### Pricing Overview (March 2026) | Plan | Price | Billed As | Savings | |------|-------|-----------|---------| | **Free (Basic)** | $0/mo | Free forever | — | | **Premium 1 Month** | $11.99/mo | $11.99 billed monthly | 0% | | **Premium 1 Year** | $4.00/mo | $47.99 billed annually | 67% | | **Premium 2 Years** | $2.99/mo | $69.99 billed every 24 months | 76% | ### What Each Tier Includes - **Basic (Free):** Free VPN with AES-256 encryption, connects to the fastest available server only (no location choice), unlimited data on mobile (500MB on desktop), no account registration required. Limited to basic VPN functionality — no kill switch, no split tunneling, no server selection. - **Premium:** Full access to 10,000+ servers across 80 countries with 250+ locations, all protocols including WireGuard and Everest, kill switch, Double VPN, ad and tracker blocker, search protection, dark web monitoring, PC cleaner utility, static IP option, and 5 simultaneous device connections. Comes with a 30-day money-back guarantee. ### Is It Worth the Price? At $2.99/month on the 2-year plan, X-VPN undercuts NordVPN ($3.39/month) and matches many mid-tier VPNs. However, Surfshark at $1.99/month offers faster speeds, unlimited simultaneous connections, and a completed independent audit — making it objectively better value. The free plan is genuinely useful for occasional, light VPN usage on mobile. Unlike many free VPNs, X-VPN does not appear to sell user data or inject ads into browsing (on the app itself; the mobile free version does show ads). The 500MB desktop limit is restrictive, but the unlimited mobile data is unusual and valuable. The 1-month plan at $11.99 is overpriced compared to competitors. NordVPN's monthly plan is $12.99 but comes with far superior speeds, audited privacy, and more advanced features. ### Payment Methods X-VPN accepts credit/debit cards, PayPal, and cryptocurrency via CoinPayments (Bitcoin, Bitcoin Cash, Ethereum). The crypto option is a plus for privacy-conscious users who want anonymous payment. --- ## Apps & Usability X-VPN is available across a wide range of platforms, and the apps are clean and beginner-friendly — though the simplicity comes at the cost of advanced configuration options. ### Supported Platforms - **Desktop:** Windows, macOS, Linux - **Mobile:** Android, iOS - **TV:** Android TV, Apple TV, Amazon Fire TV Stick - **Browser:** Chrome extension - **Other:** Routers, Chromebook, Game Consoles (PS4/PS5/Switch via router) ### Simultaneous Connections - **Free Plan:** 1 device - **Premium Plan:** 5 devices simultaneously Five simultaneous connections is below the current industry standard. NordVPN allows 10, Surfshark offers unlimited, and even budget competitors like Atlas VPN include unlimited devices. For households with multiple devices, this is a meaningful limitation. ### User Interface The desktop and mobile apps feature a clean, minimal design with a one-click connect button and a server list organized by country and purpose (streaming, torrenting). The interface is intuitive for beginners who just want to connect and go. However, the simplicity becomes a weakness for advanced users. The protocol selector shows only letter labels (Protocol A, Protocol B, etc.) without explaining what each protocol actually is. There are no advanced settings for DNS configuration, port selection, or tunneling behavior.
💡 Pro Tip: X-VPN uses its own proprietary protocol rather than industry-standard WireGuard or OpenVPN. Proprietary protocols cannot be independently audited or verified the same way open protocols can. For users who prioritize verifiable security, Mullvad VPN (WireGuard only, fully audited) or NordVPN (NordLynx/WireGuard, 6 audits) offer stronger security transparency. See our VPN protocols guide for details.
### Known Issues - **Connections lag after 15+ minutes** — Cloudwards reported that connections frequently lag after extended use, requiring reconnection - **Customer support is limited** — Live chat operates 18 hours daily, 6 days per week (reduced from 24/6). Email support has 24+ hour response times - **FAQ articles are poor quality** — The help center contains approximately 20 articles described by reviewers as "vague" and "written in broken English" - **No split tunneling** — previously available as "app control" but reportedly removed --- ## Comparison with Alternatives To put X-VPN in context, here is how it compares against two popular alternatives — one premium and one budget. | Feature | X-VPN Premium | NordVPN | Surfshark | |---------|--------------|---------|----------| | Price (best deal) | $2.99/mo (2yr) | $3.39/mo (2yr) | $1.99/mo (2yr+) | | Servers | 10,000+ | 7,000+ | 3,200+ | | Countries | 80 | 118 | 100 | | Speed (avg download) | 13.8 Mbps | 780 Mbps | 700 Mbps | | Kill Switch | Yes | Yes | Yes | | No-Logs Audits | In progress | 6 (PwC, Deloitte) | 2 (Deloitte) | | Protocols | WireGuard, OpenVPN, Everest | NordLynx, OpenVPN, IKEv2 | WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2 | | Netflix US | Yes | Yes | Yes | | BBC iPlayer | No | Yes | Yes | | Simultaneous Connections | 5 | 10 | Unlimited | | Split Tunneling | No | Yes | Yes | | Double VPN / Multi-hop | Yes | Yes | Yes | | Ad Blocker | Yes | Yes (Threat Protection) | Yes (CleanWeb) | | Free Tier | Yes (limited) | No | No | | Jurisdiction | Singapore/Hong Kong | Panama | Netherlands | | Independent Audit | Not completed | 6 completed | 2 completed | | Transparency Report | Yes (2025) | Yes | Yes | X-VPN's main advantage is having a free tier — something neither NordVPN nor Surfshark offers. But on virtually every paid comparison metric, both competitors deliver significantly more value. Surfshark at $1.99/month is $1 cheaper than X-VPN while offering faster speeds, unlimited devices, BBC iPlayer access, split tunneling, and completed independent audits. --- ## What We Do Not Like To be transparent about X-VPN's most concerning issues: 1. **Mystery protocols.** Labeling encryption protocols with letters instead of names is not a security feature — it is obfuscation. Users deserve to know whether they are connecting via WireGuard, OpenVPN, or something unverified. This is X-VPN's single biggest trust problem. 2. **No completed independent audit.** X-VPN has operated since 2017 — nine years without a completed third-party security audit. The company says one is "in progress," but competitors like NordVPN have completed six audits in the same timeframe. 3. **Plagiarized privacy policy.** Cloudwards identified that X-VPN's privacy policy appeared copied from ExpressVPN. Whether intentional or not, this raises questions about the company's commitment to crafting genuine, enforceable privacy protections. 4. **Jurisdiction confusion.** Is X-VPN based in Hong Kong or Singapore? Different official documents say different things. For a privacy product, unclear jurisdiction is a serious concern — it determines which government can compel data disclosure. 5. **Slow speeds on distant servers.** Speed drops of 70-90% on international connections make X-VPN unsuitable for users who need consistent performance across regions. 6. **Only 5 simultaneous connections.** In 2026, this is below the industry standard. Surfshark offers unlimited connections at a lower price. 7. **Degraded customer support.** Reducing live chat from 24/6 to 18/6 and having poorly written help articles suggests the company is cutting corners on user support. --- ## Who Should Use X-VPN? ### Consider X-VPN If You Are - **A mobile user who wants a free VPN** — X-VPN's free tier with unlimited mobile data and no account requirement is genuinely useful for occasional VPN needs. It is safer than many free VPN alternatives that monetize your data. - **Budget-constrained and need streaming access** — At $2.99/month, X-VPN unblocks Netflix US, Disney+, Hulu, and Amazon Prime Video. If BBC iPlayer is not a priority, it handles the major US platforms adequately. - **In a country with internet restrictions** — The Everest protocol is designed specifically for bypassing censorship, and X-VPN's large server network across 80 countries provides many options for finding an unblocked connection. ### Look Elsewhere If You Are - **Privacy-focused** — Without a completed independent audit and with a plagiarized privacy policy, X-VPN cannot match the verified trust of NordVPN, Mullvad, or Proton VPN. - **A power user** — No split tunneling, unnamed protocols, and limited configuration options make X-VPN frustrating for anyone who wants control over their VPN setup. - **Streaming BBC iPlayer** — X-VPN cannot unblock BBC iPlayer. NordVPN and Surfshark are better choices for UK content. - **In a multi-device household** — Five simultaneous connections is restrictive. Surfshark's unlimited device policy at a lower price is a better fit. - **Needing fast international speeds** — X-VPN's 70-90% speed drop on distant servers rules it out for users who need consistent global performance. --- ## Frequently Asked Questions ### Is X-VPN safe to use in 2026? X-VPN provides AES-256 encryption and passed DNS leak tests in our testing. The kill switch worked reliably when we tested it. However, significant transparency issues remain: the company uses unnamed proprietary protocols, has not completed an independent security audit, and its privacy policy was reportedly plagiarized from ExpressVPN. The 2025 Transparency Report showing zero data disclosures across 239,000+ requests is encouraging but unverified. X-VPN is likely safe for basic browsing privacy, but users with serious security needs should choose an audited provider like NordVPN or Mullvad. ### Is X-VPN really free? Yes, X-VPN offers a genuinely free tier. On mobile (Android and iOS), you get unlimited data with no account registration required. On desktop, the free tier is limited to 500MB. The free plan connects you to the fastest available server automatically — you cannot choose specific locations or countries. The free mobile version does display ads. Premium features like kill switch, server selection, Double VPN, and ad blocking are reserved for paid plans starting at $2.99/month. ### Does X-VPN work with Netflix? Yes. In our March 2026 tests, X-VPN successfully unblocked Netflix US with HD streaming quality. It also worked with Disney+, Amazon Prime Video, Hulu, and HBO Max. However, it failed to unblock BBC iPlayer across all tested UK servers. Cloudwards rated X-VPN's streaming capability at 85/100. ### What is X-VPN's Everest protocol? Everest is X-VPN's proprietary VPN protocol designed for bypassing censorship and geo-restrictions. Unlike open-source protocols such as WireGuard and OpenVPN, Everest's code is not publicly available for review. X-VPN claims it offers enhanced unblocking capabilities, but because it is closed-source, independent security researchers cannot verify its safety or identify potential vulnerabilities. This is one of our primary concerns with the service. ### Where is X-VPN based? X-VPN is operated by Lightninglink Networks Pte. Ltd., registered in Singapore. However, multiple sources describe X-VPN as being based in Hong Kong. The Terms of Service are governed by Singapore law. Neither Singapore nor Hong Kong is a member of the Five Eyes, Nine Eyes, or 14 Eyes surveillance alliances, but both jurisdictions have their own data retention and government access frameworks. ### How many devices can I use X-VPN on? The free plan supports 1 device at a time. Premium plans allow up to 5 simultaneous connections. This is below the current industry standard — NordVPN allows 10 devices, and Surfshark offers unlimited simultaneous connections. If you need to protect more than 5 devices, you can install X-VPN on a router, which counts as a single connection while protecting all devices on your network. ### Does X-VPN support torrenting? Yes. X-VPN allows P2P traffic on most of its servers, with optimized torrenting servers available in 15+ countries including the US, UK, Germany, and Canada. Only servers with the "-56" suffix do not support P2P, and there are very few of those. However, X-VPN lacks advanced P2P features like port forwarding or SOCKS5 proxy support that dedicated torrenting VPNs like Private Internet Access offer. The relatively slow upload speeds (1-9 Mbps average) may also frustrate heavy torrent users. ### Can I get a refund from X-VPN? Yes. X-VPN offers a 30-day money-back guarantee on all premium plans purchased through the official website. You can request a refund for any reason within 30 days of purchase. The company also provides 24/7 email support and live chat (18 hours daily, 6 days per week) to process refund requests.
